title: Uniface 10.4.02: Updates since 10.4.01
text: Uniface 10.4 .02: Updates since 10.4 .01 Uniface 10.4 .02 consolidates new features, bug fixes and currency updates since 10.4 .01. Documentation was updated for the following: 10.4 .01.020 There are no significant documentation updates in this patch. 10.4 .01.019 Doc Maintenance: Fallback mechanism when calling Xalan for an XML transform on Linux When using USYSXLST to transform XLM streams at runtime, Uniface calls the Xalan XSLT processor to perform this transform. A note has been added to explain how Uniface calls Xalan on Linux and its fallback mechanism. For more information, see USYSXSLT . 10.4 .01.018 Issue UNI-28229: Change in fall back behavior when using callfieldtrigger to call menu trigger If the callfieldtrigger statement tries to call a menu trigger that is not defined at field level, it falls back to the same trigger at a higher level, such as the entity, component, or application. Previously, the highest documented level was the component level. Note: If you defined an application-levelmenutrigger, this could lead to unexpected behavior if you previously relied on callfieldtrigger only falling back to a lower level. For more information, see callfieldtrigger . Doc Maintenance: Added instructions for when to use ulic /prep with Sentinel licensing When using the License Management utility ( ulic ) to activate and return Sentinel licenses, you need to use the /prep command for certain license types to prepare your system for licensing by creating persistence data. For more information, see Creating Persistence Data with ulic /prep . 10.4 .01.017 Feature: Install the RMS License Manager silently without configuring a license The Sentinel RMS License Manager and Cloud-Plug-in can now be installed non-interactively without starting the services or configuring a license, using the -installonly argument. For more information, see Silently Installing the RMS License Manager on Windows or Silently Installing the RMS License Manager on Unix and Linux . 10.4 .01.016 Feature: Property names for dimensions and position have been standardized Properties that define the size and position of frames in forms and reports are now labeled consistently in the Properties Inspector, thus Top , Left , Height , and Width , instead of X-Position , Y-Position , X-Size , and Y-Size . The documentation has been updated to reflect this change. For more information, see Height , Width , Top , and Left . 10.4 .01.015 Issue 27302: Standalone Cloud-Served Licenses are now downloaded by the Uniface Router When you install Uniface and select a standalone cloud-served license, the Uniface Router is now configured to act as the cloud-served license client that downloads the cloud-served license, and the Uniface Server(s) check out the license as a standalone license file. This improves performance in environments with multiple Uniface Servers. In upgraded versions of Uniface, you can configure this manually. To disable this feature, remove the $LICENSE_OPTIONS setting from your urouter.asn file. For more information, see Configuring Standalone Cloud-Served Licenses . Issue 27840: A component signature must be specified when generating Java files for Java call-in When generating Java files using /sto , the output file name and at least one component signature must be specified. If the format is incorrect, an error showing the correct format is displayed. For more information, see /sto . Issue 31228: New UHTTP operation GET_LAST_ERROR returns CURL error string The GET_LAST_ERROR operation allows you to troubleshoot the source of a SEND operation -12 error. For more information, see GET_LAST_ERROR . Doc Maintenance: Sentinel Licensing documentation has been improved The documentation on how to configure Sentinel licenses has been has been restructured to provide a better overview and instructions. For more information, see Uniface License Management . 10.4 .01.014 Doc maintenance: Instructions for UDDE support have been updated to use activate protocol For more information, see DDE Support and Enable DDE Support . Doc maintenance: $itemnr sets $procerror if the specified index number is not found For more information, see $itemnr . Doc maintenance: Processing Requests and Responses for Static Server Pages The documentation on web applications has been enhanced to better explain the role of the webget and webgen statements in loading and generating a Uniface Server response. For more information, see Processing Requests and Responses for Static Server Pages . Removed references to SAP HANA, which is no longer supported. Removed U_DOC row from UXGROUP and UXFIELD tables. 10.4 .01.013 Doc Maintenance: Configuring a Redundant RMS License Manager pool For the Uniface Enterprise Edition, you can ensure that your licensing system is always online by configuring redundantSentinel license servers. To do so, your Sentinel Entitlement needs to include the Redundant RMS feature. Contact your Uniface representative to add this to your network on-premises served license. For more information, see Redundant RMS License Manager Pool . Issue: UNI-27711 Unifields do not support a CellHeight property value of below 100 Setting the CellHeight value to less than 100 causes the text in Unifields to be slightly truncated at the bottom. For more information, see Cell Height (CellHeight) . Doc Maintenance: Added platform code for Microsoft Windows Server 2019 The ProcScript function $oprsys returns code T when running on Microsoft Windows Server 2019. For more information, see $oprsys . 10.4 .01.012 Doc Maintenance: Returning an Network On-Premises Served License To return an network on-premises license, use the Revoke Tool prior to uninstalling the RMS License Manager. If you uninstall your RMS License Manager or it is not running when returning your license, you need to manually revoke your license. For more information, see Returning Network On-Premises Served Licenses . Issue: UNI-27008 Line Space Window property only affects the runtime layout The Line Space property affects only the runtime layout, not the layout in the Design Frames worksheet. For more information, see Line Space (LineSpace) . Doc Maintenance: Updated instructions for using mappings with urm entitycopy For more information, see urm entitycopy . 10.4 .01.011 Currency: Uniface now supports Windows 11 Windows 11 features a new look and feel, and this has required some changes to initialization settings to ensure that existing Uniface desktop applications can run using the Windows 11 conventions. In addition, new window and widget properties have been added to provide more control when designing form components. If you intend to run your existing application on Windows 11, you will need to make a few changes to your application initialization file to ensure the best compliance with the new Windows 11 look and feel. The default usys.ini delivered in a Uniface installation has already been updated to reflect these changes. For application to look their best on Windows 11, apply the following changes in the application initialization files: Update the Windows definition in the [application] section to include the CellHeight and LineSpace properties: [application] Window=uwindow(backcolor=white;cellheight=110;linespace=0) Copy To ensure that adjacent fields do not have overlapping borders in existing applications, the recommended value of CellHeight is 110 . If you currently use a cell height of 100 , this slightly increases the size of existing forms. If this is unacceptable, you can use a cell height of 105 instead. Setting the value to less than 105 causes the text in input fields to be slightly truncated at the bottom. The values set for CellHeight and LineSpace in the initialization file are ignored for forms in which these values are specified as window properties. Remove the CellHeight and LineSpace initialization settings from the [upi] section. Note: For compatibility reasons, if the initialization settings CellHeight and LineSpace in the [upi] section are set to values other than their defaults (CellHeight=100 and Linespace=0 ), they will take precedence over the values set by the Windows setting in the initialization file. For more information, see Window . Feature: New Window properties CellHeight and LineSpace for Form components The CellHeight and LineSpace for Windows applications can now be set as Window properties. You can set them globally for the entire application using the Windows setting in the [application] section of the initialization file, or you can set them in the Windows properties of a form component. They are static properties, but the property name and value are returned by $widgetproperties . For more information, see Cell Height (CellHeight) and Line Space (LineSpace) . Feature: Widget property Draw 3-D Frame Outside available for more widgets In addition to the ComboBox and DropDown List widgets, the property Draw 3-D Frame Outside is now available for the following Form widgets: Edit Box, Output Box, Rich Edit Box, and Spin Button. It should be set to false (the default) to ensure that the 3-D border does not interfere with adjacent fields. For more information, see Draw 3-D Frame Outside (DrawOutside) . Currency: New platform codes for Windows 11 and Windows Server 2022 The ProcScript function $oprsys returns code Q when running on Windows 11, and code R when running on Windows Server 2022. For more information, see $oprsys . Issue UNI-26974: Radio Group widget enhancement For the Radio Group widget, the Transparency property should be set to false to ensure that the radio buttons are correctly displayed when running on Windows 11. If Transparency is True , the button that is selected appears hexagonal instead of round. For more information, see Transparency and uradiogroup (RadioGroup) . Feature: The Load Definitions facility is now available for DB2 The Project Editor has been enhanced to support the Load Definitions facility for DB2. This enables you to create modeled entities using the definitions of your existing DB2 database. For more information, see Load Definitions Facility for DB2 and Load Definitions Facility for DB2 on iSeries . Feature: RMS installation checks connection to the Sentinel Cloud Server When installing the Sentinel RMS License Manager with a cloud-served license and no proxy server, the script now tests the connection to the Sentinel Cloud server before continuing. If there is a problem with your connection or firewall settings, you can fix it and try again without exiting the installation script. For more information, see Installing the Sentinel RMS License Manager on Windows or Installing the Sentinel RMS License Manager on Unix and Linux . UNI-27372 OS Service UNI-27373 Repository Changes per Entity UNI-27374 More Editors 10.4 .01.010 Issue UNI-13218: Enhanced export functionality for Projects The Actions menu in the Project Editor now provides the following choices under Export Project : Project —formerly Export to XML Project & Objects —formerly Export full Project to XML Project & Objects Recursively —export all the objects in the current project, including the referenced project and its objects. This is option is only visible if the project contains reference to other projects. For more information, see Export a project . The sort/e statement is deprecated sort and sort/e are synonymous. For more information, see sort . Doc Maintenance: Updated configuring a local Sentinel License for iSeries Added steps for configuring a Sentinel local license file for iSeries. For more information, see Configure a Sentinel License on iSeries . 10.4 .01.009 Feature: Activate Multiple Entitlements on RMS for Unix Activating multiple Entitlement IDs is now possible using the Sentinel RMS License Manager Installer on Unix and Linux. For more information, see Installing the Sentinel RMS License Manager on Unix and Linux . 10.4 .01.008 Issue UNI-24897: Informix connector supports maximum key length of 511 The maximum key length supported by the Informix database connector has been increased to 511 for Informix OL. However, to use this new maximum key length, the page size used by the database needs to be at least 4K. For more information, see DBMS Connector Limits . Doc Maintenance: $webinfo("WebServerContext") The previously undocumented REQUESTTYPE and EXPECTEDRESPONSE properties returned by $webinfo("WebServerContext") are deprecated and should not be used. They do not provide the proper information for contained DSP instances. Use $webrequesttype and $webresponsetype instead. For more information, see $webinfo ("WebServerContext") . 10.4 .01.007 Doc Maintenance: Updates for active path documentation The documentation about the active path has been rewritten and now includes information about the effect that collection and occurrence operations can have on the active path. For more information, see Active Path for Runtime Processing . Doc Maintenance: Updated $webrequesttype and $webresponsetype The meaning of the values returned by these functions has been more precisely defined. For more information, see $webrequesttype and $webresponsetype . Feature: Activate Multiple Entitlements on RMS for Windows Activating multiple Entitlement IDs is now possible using the Sentinel RMS License Manager Installer on Windows. For more information, see Installing the Sentinel RMS License Manager on Windows . 10.4 .01.006 Feature: New $ude("export") options to export referenced objects and projects You can now export all referenced objects of a project using the OptionList parameter FullProject . If a project includes a reference to another project, you can export this as part of the current project using the RecursiveProject OptionList parameter. For more information, see $ude ("export") . Feature: New option in $LICENSE_OPTIONS for the end of a maintenance contract Users will now be notified if their maintenance contract is coming to an end, or if it has ended. These messages can be managed using $LICENSE_OPTIONS with LM_MAINTENANCE_NOTIFICATION . Messages can be customized using the LM_NOTIFICATION_POSTMESSAGE maintenance and no_more_maintenance REASON options. For more information, see $LICENSE_OPTIONS . Doc Maintenance: Creating UARs using ProcScript Code examples have been added to the documentation on creating Uniface Archive (UAR) files using ProcScript. For more information, see Create Uniface Archive Files and $ude ("archive") . Doc Maintenance: Java Call-In API no longer supports remote message invocation (RMI) The Java Call-In API no longer supports remote message invocation (RMI), and the simple RMI server previously provided by Uniface is no longer supplied. The documentation has been updated to reflect this change. For more information, see Java Call-in API . 10.4 .01.005 Feature: Reusing an existing Standalone license when installing the Enterprise Edition When installing Uniface using the Windows installer, you can choose to re-use an existing standalone license (cloud-served or on-premises) license, if it is activated and in the default location. To do so, choose the installer option I will configure manually later, or use existing Standalone licensing . Uniface will use this activated license to proceed with the installation. For more information, see Install the Uniface Enterprise Edition on Windows . Issue: Uniface now starts before a cloud-served license file is updated Updates to the Entitlement of a cloud-served license will not take effect the first time you start your Uniface application. Start and stop your application or remove the license file to reflect the changes to your Entitlement. For more information, see Standalone Cloud-Served License . Doc maintenance: Documentation on deploying applications has been improved Revised and added topics include Deployment Process and Create Uniface Archive Files . For more information, see Deploying Applications . 10.4 .01.004 Currency: Uniface now supports Java Call-In on Linux Uniface now supports Java Call-In on Linux. For more information, see Java Call-In . Feature: OSCommand string of the spawn statement can now be up to 2047 bytes For more information, see spawn . Doc Maintenance: Added Error Handling for UPOPMAIL For more information, see UPOPMAIL . 10.4 .01.003 Feature: Activating multiple Entitlement IDs The Uniface License Management Utility now allows you to activate multiple Entitlement IDs. On Windows, you can use the License Activation utility from the Start menu for standalone cloud-served licenses. On all platforms, you can use the Uniface License Management command line utility ( ulic ) with the /act command. Each Entitlement ID must be added and activated one at a time. For more information, see Uniface License Management Utility (ulic) . Feature: Manually configure licensing for RMS License Manager later When installing the RMS License Manager, you can choose to configure your license manually later. The RMS License Manager and the Sentinel Cloud Plug-in will be installed but not started. For more information, see Installing the Sentinel RMS License Manager on Windows or Installing the Sentinel RMS License Manager on Unix and Linux . 10.4 .01.002 Feature: Uniface Server users have privileges to the cloud-served license folder The Uniface License Management utility grants all users full privileges to the license folder to allow Uniface Server users to update a cloud-served license file. If you change USYSLIC to point to a folder of your own choice, you must start the Uniface License Management utility with an account that has permission to access that folder, or the Uniface Server will not renew the cloud-served license. For more information, see USYS Path Logicals . Feature: Import errors report an exit status When using the /imp command line switch, an exit code of 1 ( EXIT_FAILURE ) is returned if the import process fails. In this case, the transcript window or log file provides more information about the failure. For more information, see /imp . Currency: Java Call-In supported on Windows 10 Uniface now supports Java Call-In on Windows 10. The documentation on how to call Uniface components from Java has also been rewritten to provide a better overview and instructions. For more information, see Java Call-In . Currency: PouchDB has been upgraded to a more secure version The PouchDB JavaScript database used for offline storage in DSPs has been upgraded from version 5.4.5 to 7.2.1. Prior to version 7.0.0, PouchDB included default adapters for IndexedDB and the deprecated WebSQL database. The actual adapter used was handled transparently, depending on the platform and browser. The latest version only includes the IndexedDB adapter. For more information, see Client-Side Data Storage . Currency: C/C++ compilers for 3GL integration When compiling C or C++ components that integrate with Uniface, we recommend using the same compiler (or a compliant compiler) as is used to compile the Uniface sources. The compilers used depend on the operating system and are listed in the Platform Availability Matrix . For more information, see Compiling and Linking 3GL . Doc Maintenance: Setting WORKDIR with $setting When addressing Uniface runtime settings using $setting , WORKDIR can be a relative path. For more information, see Addressing Runtime Settings with $setting . Doc Maintenance: Default setting for $ENHANCED_EDIT The default value for $ENHANCED_EDIT is 0 (No option enabled). For more information, see $ENHANCED_EDIT . Doc Maintenance: Oracle Service Stored Procedures Added missing information on support for Service Stored Procedures (SSP). For more information, see Service Stored Procedures Support on Oracle . 10.4 .01.001 Feature: Friendly name can be added for cloud-served licenses When activating a standalone cloud-served license, you can add a friendly name for your machine, which is used to recognize the machine in the Sentinel EMS portal. You can edit the friendly name when activating your Sentinel license. For more information, see Standalone Cloud-Served License and Uniface License Management Utility (ulic) . Issue: Updated information about the use of $RES with ProcScript Statements The resources deployment path ($RES) cannot be used with dircreate . When using $RES with dirrename or filerename , only use $RES with the first parameter. For more information, see dircreate , dirrename , and filerename . Issue: Updated Popup Rectangle (PopupRect) property The Popup Rectangle (Popup Rect) property affects all popup forms anchored to a field. For more information, see Popup Rectangle (PopupRect) . Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
